items.title: Secure LiteSpeed Admin Panel
items.description: Change default admin port (7080) and restrict access by IP. Enable HTTPS for admin console.

implementation.litespeed:
Admin Console > Configuration > Listeners > Admin HTTPS

# Add to vhconf.xml:
<security>
  <accessControl>
    <allow>YOUR_IP</allow>
    <deny>ALL</deny>
  </accessControl>
</security>

items.verification: Admin panel accessible only from whitelisted IPs on HTTPS

items.title: Configure Strong SSL/TLS Settings
items.description: Disable SSLv3, TLS 1.0/1.1. Enable TLS 1.2/1.3 only with strong cipher suites.

implementation.litespeed:
Admin > Configuration > SSL > SSL Protocol

sslProtocol 24

ciphers HIGH:!aNULL:!MD5:!RC4
enableECDHE 1
enforceStrongCipher 1

items.verification: SSL Labs test shows A+ rating with TLS 1.2+ only

items.title: Enable LiteSpeed WAF and reCAPTCHA
items.description: Enable built-in Web Application Firewall and configure reCAPTCHA for brute force protection.
items.verification: WAF blocks common attacks like SQLi/XSS. reCAPTCHA appears after failed logins

items.title: Secure Cache Configuration
items.description: Configure ESI (Edge Side Includes) securely. Do not cache sensitive pages. Set proper cache keys.

implementation.litespeed:
Admin > Cache > Store Settings

esiEnabled 1
esiCacheControl 0

# In .htaccess for sensitive paths:
<IfModule Litespeed>
  CacheDisable public /wp-admin
  CacheDisable public /administrator
  CacheDisable public /user/login
</IfModule>

items.verification: Sensitive pages not cached. Cache hits only on public content

items.title: Enable DDoS Protection
items.description: Configure connection limits, bandwidth throttling, and request rate limiting to prevent DDoS attacks.

implementation.litespeed:
Admin > Configuration > Server > Security

connectionLimit 10000
maxConnectionsPerIP 100
maxReqsPerSec 50
banPeriod 300

# Virtual host level:
<security>
  <accessControl>
    <denyBot>badbot</denyBot>
    <denyUserAgent>scanner</denyUserAgent>
  </accessControl>
</security>

items.verification: Server remains responsive under load. Suspicious IPs automatically banned

items.title: Configure Security Headers
items.description: Add X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection, X-Content-Type-Options, CSP headers via LiteSpeed.

implementation.litespeed:
Admin > Configuration > Server > Script Handler

# In .htaccess:
<IfModule mod_headers.c>
  Header always set X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN"
  Header always set X-XSS-Protection "1; mode=block"
  Header always set X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff"
  Header always set Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin"
  Header always set Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'"
</IfModule>

items.verification: securityheaders.com shows all headers configured correctly

items.title: Hide Server Version Information
items.description: Disable server version banner in HTTP headers to prevent information disclosure.

implementation.litespeed:
Admin > Configuration > Server > General Settings

# In httpd_config.conf:
serverSignature Off
serverTokens ProductOnly

# Or hide completely:
<IfModule mod_headers.c>
  Header unset Server
  Header unset X-Powered-By
</IfModule>

items.verification: curl -I shows no Server header or only 'Server: LiteSpeed' without version

items.title: Secure File Permissions
items.description: Ensure LiteSpeed directories and configuration files have proper permissions. Protect logs and cache.

implementation.litespeed:
# /usr/local/lsws ownership
chown -R lsadm:lsadm /usr/local/lsws/conf
chmod 750 /usr/local/lsws/conf
chmod 600 /usr/local/lsws/conf/*.conf

# Logs directory
chown -R nobody:nobody /usr/local/lsws/logs
chmod 755 /usr/local/lsws/logs

# Cache directory
chown -R nobody:nobody /usr/local/lsws/cache
chmod 755 /usr/local/lsws/cache
